content: If we map the human language learning process of repeated vocabulary reflection (from the high school English vocabulary context of reflexion) to LLM iterative output improvement, we can build a human-inspired weak-supervision refinement framework that uses spaced repetition of lexical reflection cues to guide Self-Refine iterations. This framework requires no external human feedback, only leveraging the model’s implicit memory of lexical semantic conventions accumulated through repeated reflection to polish output.
